# Quiz — Phd courses/Information theory for natural scientists/Quizzes/ITFNS Practice Quiz 3 - Questions.md

## Q1 maximal useful work
The maximal useful work extractable from a system held at reservoir temperature T is the drop in its

- A. energy E, since all of the internal energy converts to work
- B. free energy F = E − TS, the available energy at fixed T
- C. entropy S, once multiplied by the reservoir temperature T

## Q2 additivity of Boltzmann entropy
Boltzmann's entropy S = ln Ω is additive over independent subsystems because

- A. the temperatures of the subsystems become equal at equilibrium
- B. the energies of the two subsystems simply add up together
- C. the state counts multiply, Ω = Ω₁Ω₂, and log of a product adds

## Q3 negative absolute temperature
Negative absolute temperature can arise in a system whose

- A. energy spectrum is bounded above, allowing inverted populations
- B. kinetic energy is unbounded, so any high energy is available
- C. parts have been cooled well below their own freezing point

## Q4 three bits
3 bits of information shrink the space of possibilities

- A. 6 times, adding the three binary choices up together
- B. 9 times, taking the square of the three-bit count
- C. 8 times, each bit halving the space, so that 2³ = 8

## Q5 typical sequences
For a long sequence from an alphabet with entropy S per symbol, the number of typical sequences grows like

- A. 2^{NS}, far fewer than the 2^N total for unequal symbols
- B. 2^N, the same count whatever the symbol statistics are
- C. n^N, so that all sequences of that length count as typical

## Q6 conditional entropy
The conditional entropy S(B|A) is equal to

- A. S(A) + S(B), the sum of the two separate entropies
- B. S(B) − S(A), the difference of the two separate entropies
- C. S(A,B) − S(A), the joint entropy less that of the condition

## Q7 fully noisy channel
A binary symmetric channel that flips each bit with probability 1/2 has capacity

- A. 1 bit, since it still passes one bit on every single use
- B. 0, since output and input are statistically independent
- C. 1/2 bit, since half of the bits still get through correctly

## Q8 demon's cost
For the demon, the energetic cost of a full measurement-and-erasure cycle is bounded below by

- A. the free-energy change of the working gas taken by itself
- B. T times the system–memory mutual information that is built up
- C. zero, since the measure and erase steps cancel out exactly

## Q9 information is life
In the "information is life" picture, natural selection is best described as

- A. an ever more efficient encoding of the environment in the genes
- B. the survival of the strongest organisms within a population
- C. a random drift of gene frequencies that carries no information

## Q10 Kelly gambling
In proportional ("Kelly") gambling, to maximize long-run growth you bet on each outcome

- A. a share equal to that outcome's own true probability pᵢ
- B. all of it on the single most probable outcome each round
- C. an equal share on every outcome, so as to hedge one's risk

## Q11 thermodynamics predicts no numbers
Thermodynamics does not predict absolute numerical values; it sets inequalities and relations among quantities that follow from conservation laws.

- True
- False

## Q12 entropy at equilibrium
For a closed system, the entropy reaches its minimum in thermal equilibrium.

- True
- False

## Q13 entropy and certainty
The entropy −Σ pᵢ log pᵢ is maximal when one outcome is certain.

- True
- False

## Q14 sign of mutual information
Mutual information I(A;B) can become negative when A and B happen to be anticorrelated.

- True
- False

## Q15 channel capacity
Which statements about channel capacity are correct?
*(select all that apply)*

- A. it is the mutual information maximized over the input statistics
- B. it is the top rate of asymptotically error-free transmission
- C. it is simply the raw output entropy S(B) taken on its own

---
> [!answer]- Answer key
> 1. **B** — ITFNS Ch. 1
> 2. **C** — ITFNS Ch. 1
> 3. **A** — ITFNS Ch. 1
> 4. **C** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 5. **A** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 6. **C** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 7. **B** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 8. **B** — ITFNS Ch. 3
> 9. **A** — ITFNS Ch. 3
> 10. **A** — ITFNS Ch. 3
> 11. **True** — ITFNS Ch. 1
> 12. **False** — ITFNS Ch. 1
> 13. **False** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 14. **False** — ITFNS Ch. 2
> 15. **A and B** — ITFNS Ch. 2
